---
title: "Overview"
---

## Motivation

Implementing discrete-symmetry constraints — identical particles, charge
conjugation, and CP — in helicity-formalism amplitudes for cascade decays is
notoriously error-prone. The helicity amplitude carries explicit spin indices,
so it is *not* an eigenstate of particle permutation; swapping two particles
changes the kinematic point *and* mixes helicity components with non-trivial
phases. Every practical analysis (three-pion systems, $\Xi_b^-\to pK^-K^-$,
$B_s^0\to J/\psi\, p\bar p$, $J/\psi\to \eta\, p\bar p$,
$\chi_{c0,2}\to K\bar K\pi\pi$) has required solving essentially the same
problem from scratch, each time in slightly different conventions.

This report collects the rules once and for all: clear recipes for imposing
permutation symmetry and charge conjugation in the helicity formalism,
systematic cross-check methods against covariant (tensor) amplitudes, and a
registry of the conventions where sign mistakes hide.

## Strategy

Reasoning directly with helicity *state vectors* $|p; j,\lambda\rangle$ and
their transformation properties is deliberately postponed. It is hard to track
all phase conventions at the state level, and easy to arrive at contradictions.
Instead the working object is the **practical amplitude**: a concrete complex
function of four-vectors carrying helicity labels,
$$
M_{\lambda_0;\,\lambda_1\dots\lambda_n}(p_1,\dots,p_n),
$$
evaluated by explicit code (chain amplitudes with Wigner rotations, or tensor
contractions with explicit spinors/polarization vectors).

The anchoring principle of the report (stated precisely in
[the permutation chapter](02-permutations.qmd)) is:

> **Impose symmetries on the fixed-frame amplitude; derive chain-level rules by
> tracking alignment rotations.**
>
> When all helicities are defined in one common frame, particle exchange is a
> pure slot swap of $(p_i,\lambda_i)$ pairs (with the statistics sign), and
> charge conjugation touches neither momenta nor spin labels. All non-trivial
> phases — such as $(-1)^{\lambda_0\pm\lambda_2}$ in $\Xi_b^-\to pK^-K^-$ —
> arise *only* when the amplitude is re-expressed in chain-specific aligned
> frames (Dalitz-plot-decomposition style). They are Wigner-rotation
> bookkeeping, not new physics input.

This turns each cumbersome case into a two-step procedure: (1) write the
symmetry constraint in the fixed frame, where it is trivial; (2) translate to
the aligned/chain representation used by the fitter, computing the induced
phases once, explicitly, and verifying them numerically.

## Case studies

Ordered by increasing complexity of the symmetry structure:

| # | System | Symmetry content | Status |
|---|--------|------------------|--------|
| 1 | [$\pi^+\pi^-\pi^-$, $D^0D^0\pi^+$](cases/01-three-pions/notes.qmd) | identical spinless particles | mostly understood; LS-basis exchange phase to pin down |
| 2 | [$\Xi_b^-\to pK^-K^-$](cases/02-xib2pKK/notes.qmd) | identical bosons + fermion spectator, weak decay | master formula derived by closure and cross-checked; prior exponent convention still to map |
| 3 | [$J/\psi\to \eta\, p\bar p$](cases/03-jpsi2etappbar/notes.qmd) | strong decay: C, P both good — clean playground | to do |
| 4 | [$B_s^0\to J/\psi\, p\bar p$](cases/04-bs2jpsippbar/notes.qmd) | weak decay; effective C from gluonic $p\bar p$ production | cross-checked vs tensors; to revisit |
| 5 | [$\chi_{c0,2}\to K\bar K\pi\pi$](cases/05-chic2KKpipi/notes.qmd) | 4-body, C=+, $K_{1A}/K_{1B}$ mixing | open |

Each case study lives in its own subfolder under `cases/`, where additional
notes, derivations, and check scripts accumulate.

## Chapter guide

1. [Conventions and notation](01-conventions.qmd) — the fixed-frame amplitude,
   chain amplitudes, and the convention registry.
2. [Permutation symmetry](02-permutations.qmd) — the master rule, LS-basis and
   helicity-basis exchange phases, alignment-induced phases.
3. [Charge conjugation and CP](03-charge-conjugation.qmd) — C as an internal
   symmetry, current-level signs, CP relations for weak decays.
4. [Cross-check methodology](04-cross-checks.qmd) — tensor-amplitude bridge,
   numerical symmetry tests.
5. [Recipes](05-recipes.qmd) — the distilled step-by-step procedures.
6. [Claims ledger](99-ledger.qmd) — every non-trivial statement with its
   verification status.
7. [Technical appendix](appendix-microscopic.qmd) — the microscopic derivation
   of the permutation theorem image for $\Xi_b^-\to pK^-K^-$.

## Related material in this repository

- `docs/isospin-kaon-conventions.qmd` — isospin doublet conventions and
  current-level charge-conjugation signs for kaons, including the
  $K_{1A}/K_{1B}$ discussion relevant for case 5.
- `docs/four-pion-model.qmd` — the four-pion cascade model tutorial.
- `ThreeBodyDecays.jl` / `CascadeDecays.jl` — numerical implementations used
  for the cross-checks.

## Rendering

```sh
cd symmetry_report
quarto render        # -> _report/index.html
quarto preview       # live preview while editing
```
